extraction_notes: Near-perfect agreement with ANZG PCx table. Reference conditions: pH 7.0 / 20°C / total ammonia-N.
ANZECC 2000 trigger is unconfirmed; ANZG 2025 DGV likely differs. HC5 ~2.4× published 700 µg/L.

published_source: ANZECC 2000 (col-49 label error corrected to mg/L)
extraction_notes: ANZG 2021 dataset is larger than ANZECC 2000; HC5 ~2.4× the ANZECC trigger. May reflect newer data. Verify ANZG 2021 technical brief DGV.

published_source: ANZG 2026 technical brief — DGV not extracted
extraction_notes: No published DGV available in extracted data. Read ANZG 2026 Cr technical brief to verify.
Excellent agreement (our 0.42 vs ANZG 0.47 µg/L at DOC=0.5 mg/L). Small difference from model averaging vs ANZG SSD implementation.
Our SSD uses raw uncorrected concentrations; ANZECC 2000 trigger includes pH/BLM correction. HC5 difference expected without correction. Verify ANZG 2024 draft DGV at reference conditions.

Our SSD uses MLR-normalised EC10s at reference conditions (DOC 0.5 mg/L / hardness 30 / pH 7.5). ANZECC 2000 trigger at hardness <30 mg/L. Differences reflect updated dataset and conditions. Verify ANZG 2024 draft DGV.

Expected HC5(acute) ≈ 55 × 10 = 550 µg/L; our HC5 = 245 µg/L (0.45×). Possible data extraction issue or different ACR. The LR pH<6.5 trigger is 0.8 µg/L (entirely separate — our SSD is for pH>6.5 data only). Check Warne 2000 Al section (early pages of toxicant-trigger-value-derivation_0.pdf).
HR analyte: our HC5 should ≈ 24 µg/L but is 61 µg/L (2.6×). Could reflect mixed As(III)/As(V) data in extraction or different species selection. Check As section in Warne 2000.
HR analyte. Our HC5 = 0.12 vs published 0.2 µg/L (0.6×). Within acceptable range given different SSD fitting method. Published 99% = 0.06 µg/L; our HC1 = 0.013 µg/L (more conservative).
HR analyte. Our HC5 = 4.04 vs published 3.4 µg/L (1.2×). Good agreement.
Expected HC5(acute) = 1900 × 9.1 = 17290 µg/L. Our HC5 = 13448 µg/L (0.78×). Good agreement confirming extraction is correct.
HR analyte. Our HC5 = 1.13 vs published 0.6 µg/L (1.9×). Note: published trigger is for inorganic Hg; our data from Warne 2000 may mix inorganic and total Hg forms.

Expected HC5(acute) = 0.2 × 25 = 5 µg/L. Our HC5 = 1.34 µg/L (0.27×). Our model gives a lower (more conservative) HC5. Warne 2000 data may have missed some less sensitive species or used different data. Check lindane section in Warne 2000.

Dieldrin is LR in ANZECC 2000 (AF=100 applied to lowest acute value), NOT SSD-derived. Our SSD was fitted to 7 values from Warne 2000 but these were not considered sufficient for SSD by ANZECC. HC5 = 0.185 vs published 0.01 µg/L (18.5×). The LR trigger reflects acute data ÷ 100, not an HC5. This analyte should be treated with low confidence.
Excellent agreement. Expected HC5(acute) = 0.02 × 10 = 0.2 µg/L. Our HC5 = 0.192 µg/L (0.96×). Extraction confirmed accurate.
Expected HC5(acute) = 0.01 × 71 = 0.71 µg/L. Our HC5 = 1.71 µg/L (2.4×). Moderate discrepancy; data extraction may be slightly off or different species used. Check DDT section in Warne 2000.
Excellent agreement. Expected HC5(acute) = 0.01 × 17.5 = 0.175 µg/L. Our HC5 = 0.194 µg/L (1.1×). Extraction confirmed accurate.

Expected HC5(acute) = 0.05 × 17.5 = 0.875 µg/L. Our HC5 = 267 µg/L (305×). Almost certain PDF extraction error: raw values (260–14163) appear to be in µg/L but the Warne 2000 PDF likely reported them in mg/L. Dividing by 1000 gives HC5 ≈ 0.27 µg/L (closer to expected 0.875). CHECK: Warne 2000 malathion section — confirm units (mg/L or µg/L).

HR analyte: our HC5 = 0.033 vs published 0.01 µg/L (3.3×). Could be different species/study selection rather than extraction error. Chlorpyrifos chronic data is well-studied. Check Warne 2000 chlorpyrifos section.

Parathion MR trigger = HC5 from acute SSD directly (no separate ACR stated). Expected HC5 ≈ 0.004 µg/L. Our HC5 = 49.2 µg/L (12300×). Definite extraction error. Typical parathion acute LC50 for sensitive invertebrates: 0.001–0.1 µg/L; for fish: 10–500 µg/L. Our values (65–28896 µg/L) include no sensitive invertebrate data and values 100–10000× too high. CHECK: Warne 2000 parathion section — likely wrong values extracted or units error.

Expected HC5(acute) = 0.02 × 10 = 0.2 µg/L. Our HC5 = 1.69 µg/L (8.5×). Likely extraction error or values missing the most sensitive species (crustaceans typically LC50 0.01–0.1 µg/L). CHECK: Warne 2000 azinphos-methyl section.
Expected HC5(acute) = 0.15 × 10 = 1.5 µg/L. Our HC5 = 5.1 µg/L (3.4×). Moderate discrepancy. CHECK: Warne 2000 dimethoate section.

Published trigger = 16 µg/L (MR); if ACR=10 default, HC5(acute) ≈ 160 µg/L. Our HC5 = 75 µg/L (0.47×). Within 2× if ACR was less than 10. Reasonable — not flagged as error. Check Warne 2000 naphthalene section to confirm ACR used.

BaP water solubility ≈ 3–6 µg/L. Our extracted values (72–1650 µg/L) exceed aqueous solubility — physically impossible. Values almost certainly in ng/L not µg/L. Dividing by 1000: HC5 ≈ 0.09 µg/L (closer to published 0.2 µg/L). CHECK: Warne 2000 benzo[a]pyrene section — confirm units. Model is invalid until corrected.

Published ANZECC 2000 benzene trigger not extracted from available HTML. ACR=10 stated in HTML. Expected HC5(acute) should be trigger × 10. Read ANZECC 2000 Table 3.4.1 (vol1 p. ~360) for trigger value.

Published ANZECC 2000 o-xylene trigger not extracted from available HTML. Read ANZECC 2000 Table 3.4.1. QSAR alternative 95% trigger for m-xylene = 75 µg/L (HTML); o-xylene listed separately in Table 3.4.1.
Expected HC5(acute) = 320 × 16.3 = 5216 µg/L. Our HC5 = 2941 µg/L (0.56×). Within 2× — extraction confirmed reasonable.

HTML reports 2-CP at 99% protection = 340 µg/L; 95% protection > 340 µg/L. Our HC5 = 4598 µg/L (~10–13× too high). Likely extraction error — values (5236–20473 µg/L) are for much less sensitive species or wrong section. CHECK: Warne 2000 chlorophenols section.

published_source: ANZECC 2000 Table 3.4.1 — DGV not extracted from HTML
extraction_notes: 2,4,6-TCP MR trigger value not extracted from available HTML. Read ANZECC 2000 Table 3.4.1 or Warne 2000 chlorophenols section.

published_source: ANZECC 2000 HTML (MR — HR HC5=24 µg/L but 10 µg/L MR trigger used)
extraction_notes: HR SSD HC5 = 24 µg/L (our value 9.23 µg/L, 0.38×). ANZECC chose more conservative MR trigger of 10 µg/L (ACR=4.5 on acute HC5=45 µg/L). Our HC5 of 9.23 µg/L using the 11 chronic data points is very close to the published 10 µg/L trigger — good agreement.
